summary: Task 2 Gradle infrastructure implementation satisfies all 12 acceptance criteria: versions match tech-spec Decision T2, plugins apply false are complete, Hilt wired through KSP (no kapt anywhere), both module build scripts carry the correct namespace/SDK/jvmTarget/context-receivers config, libs.versions.toml is structured correctly, wrapper targets Gradle 8.11.1, and no forbidden deps (Firebase/oss-licenses/DataStore/protobuf/camera/richtext/OAuth/security-crypto/room/moshi/mockk) are present. Smoke builds pass locally. A handful of minor/nit observations below, none blocking.

findings.issue: hilt-android + ksp(hilt-android-compiler) added here although task spec deferred runtime Hilt deps to Task 3. Documented in the review request as a known deviation forced by the hilt-application Gradle plugin failing configuration-phase without at least a stub hilt-android on the classpath.
findings.suggestion: Accept as-is. Record this deviation in decisions.md (Post-completion checklist item of Task 2) so Task 3 knows the runtime dep is already present and does not double-add.

findings.issue: Uses legacy kotlinOptions { jvmTarget = "11"; freeCompilerArgs += "-Xcontext-receivers" } DSL. Kotlin 2.2 emits deprecation warnings 'jvmTarget: String' is deprecated and 'freeCompilerArgs: List<String>' is deprecated; the replacement is kotlin { compilerOptions { jvmTarget.set(JvmTarget.JVM_11); freeCompilerArgs.add("-Xcontext-receivers") } }. Non-blocking for Kotlin 2.2.x (old DSL still functional); removal is scheduled for Kotlin 2.3+.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable for now — task spec explicitly dictates the kotlinOptions form. Track as tech-debt and migrate to the modern compilerOptions DSL when Kotlin is bumped to 2.3+ or when deprecation warnings block CI.

findings.issue: Same deprecated kotlinOptions { jvmTarget = "11" } DSL as :app. Same Kotlin 2.2 deprecation warning.
findings.suggestion: Same as above — migrate to kotlin { compilerOptions { ... } } together with :app when bumping Kotlin.

findings.issue: kotlin-reflect = 2.2.21 is declared and the library alias is present (line 37) but no module actually consumes implementation(libs.kotlin.reflect). Tech-spec § Dependencies explicitly flags this as a maybe-transitive-only pin: 'если ни один implementation/runtimeOnly путь её не требует — убрать в последующей итерации.'
findings.suggestion: Leave in place for now (AC-3 requires the version pin). Revisit in Task 3: once real code in :core-runtime is in place, run ./gradlew :core-runtime:dependencies and drop kotlin-reflect from libs.versions.toml if nothing resolves to it.

findings.issue: kotlinx-serialization-json = 1.7.3 is referenced in tech-spec Decision T2 list of pinned versions but is absent from libs.versions.toml and from the task-2 AC-3 list. The task file's AC is authoritative, so this is not an AC failure — just a tech-spec/task-spec inconsistency worth flagging.
findings.suggestion: If future tasks do not actually introduce kotlinx-serialization (tech-spec § Dependencies does not mention it either — only Decision T2), drop the pin from Decision T2 in a docs patch. Otherwise add versions.kotlinSerialization + library alias when the first consumer appears.

findings.issue: gradle.properties is minimal (jvmargs, useAndroidX, code style, nonTransitiveRClass). Reasonable defaults for Phase 1. Not a spec requirement but worth noting: no android.defaults.buildfeatures.buildconfig=false (harmless — :app opts in explicitly), no Kotlin incremental/parallel flags, no configuration cache opt-in.
findings.suggestion: Optional: once build performance matters, consider org.gradle.parallel=true, org.gradle.caching=true, and org.gradle.configuration-cache=true. No action for Task 2.

findings.issue: pluginManagement.repositories.google is constrained by includeGroupByRegex to com.android.*, com.google.*, androidx.*. This mirrors Gallery exactly and is safe here, but note: if a future plugin needs an artifact from google() outside those prefixes it will silently fall through to mavenCentral/gradlePluginPortal.
findings.suggestion: No action — correct per task 'скопировать pluginManagement ... из gallery-source'. Just be aware when adding new plugins.

findings.issue: The wrapper jar is committed as a binary (declared as binary in .gitattributes line 32 — good). Cannot verify its checksum or provenance from source files alone. Task description says it can be copied 1-to-1 from gallery-source or generated via 'gradle wrapper --gradle-version 8.11.1'.
findings.suggestion: Ensure the jar's SHA-256 matches the official https://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.11.1-wrapper.jar.sha256 artifact. Add this as a one-shot verification step (e.g., a note in decisions.md) so future contributors can validate.
